parameters.label: Printer technology
parameters.description: Printer technology

parameters.label: Bed exclude area
parameters.description: Unprintable area in XY plane. For example, X1 Series printers use the front left corner to cut filament during filament change. The area is expressed as polygon by points in following format: \

parameters.label: Elephant foot compensation
parameters.description: Shrink the initial layer on build plate to compensate for elephant foot effect

parameters.label: Elephant foot compensation layers
parameters.description: The number of layers on which the elephant foot compensation will be active. The first layer will be shrunk by the elephant foot compensation value, then the next layers will be linearly shrunk less, up to the layer indicated by this value.

parameters.label: Layer height
parameters.description: Slicing height for each layer. Smaller layer height means more accurate and more printing time

parameters.label: Printable height
parameters.description: Maximum printable height which is limited by mechanism of printer

parameters.label: Preferred orientation
parameters.description: Automatically orient stls on the Z-axis upon initial import

parameters.label: Use 3rd-party print host
parameters.description: Allow controlling BambuLab's printer through 3rd party print hosts

parameters.label: Hostname, IP or URL
parameters.description: Orca Slicer can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field should contain the hostname, IP address or URL of the printer host instance. Print host behind HAProxy with basic auth enabled can be accessed by putting the user name and password into the URL in the following format: https://username:password@your-octopi-address/

parameters.label: Device UI
parameters.description: Specify the URL of your device user interface if it's not same as print_host

parameters.label: API Key / Password
parameters.description: Orca Slicer can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field should contain the API Key or the password required for authentication.

parameters.label: Printer
parameters.description: Name of the printer

parameters.label: HTTPS CA File
parameters.description: Custom CA certificate file can be specified for HTTPS OctoPrint connections, in crt/pem format. If left blank, the default OS CA certificate repository is used.

parameters.label: Ignore HTTPS certificate revocation checks
parameters.description: Ignore HTTPS certificate revocation checks in case of missing or offline distribution points. One may want to enable this option for self signed certificates if connection fails.

parameters.label: Printer preset names
parameters.description: Names of presets related to the physical printer

parameters.label: Avoid crossing wall
parameters.description: Detour and avoid to travel across wall which may cause blob on surface

parameters.label: Avoid crossing wall - Max detour length
parameters.description: Maximum detour distance for avoiding crossing wall. Don't detour if the detour distance is large than this value. Detour length could be specified either as an absolute value or as percentage (for example 50%) of a direct travel path. Zero to disable

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured Cool Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Engineering Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the High Temp Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured PEI Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate SuperTack

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured Cool Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Engineering Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the High Temp Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured PEI Plate

parameters.label: Bed type
parameters.description: Bed types supported by the printer

parameters.label: The number of other layers print sequence
Before layer change G-code
parameters.description: This G-code is inserted at every layer change before lifting z

parameters.label: Bottom shell layers
parameters.description: This is the number of solid layers of bottom shell, including the bottom surface layer. When the thickness calculated by this value is thinner than bottom shell thickness, the bottom shell layers will be increased

parameters.label: Bottom shell thickness
parameters.description: The number of bottom solid layers is increased when slicing if the thickness calculated by bottom shell layers is thinner than this value. This can avoid having too thin shell when layer height is small. 0 means that this setting is disabled and thickness of bottom shell is absolutely determined by bottom shell layers

parameters.label: Apply gap fill
parameters.description: Enables gap fill for the selected solid surfaces. The minimum gap length that will be filled can be controlled from the filter out tiny gaps option below. Options: 1. Everywhere: Applies gap fill to top, bottom and internal solid surfaces for maximum strength 2. Top and Bottom surfaces: Applies gap fill to top and bottom surfaces only, balancing print speed, reducing potential over extrusion in the solid infill and making sure the top and bottom surfaces have no pin hole gaps 3. Nowhere: Disables gap fill for all solid infill areas. Note that if using the classic perimeter generator, gap fill may also be generated between perimeters, if a full width line cannot fit between them. That perimeter gap fill is not controlled by this setting. If you would like all gap fill, including the classic perimeter generated one, removed, set the filter out tiny gaps value to a large number, like 999999. However this is not advised, as gap fill between perimeters is contributing to the model's strength. For models where excessive gap fill is generated between perimeters, a better option would be to switch to the arachne wall generator and use this option to control whether the cosmetic top and bottom surface gap fill is generated

parameters.label: Force cooling for overhangs and bridges
parameters.description: Enable this option to allow adjustment of the part cooling fan speed for specifically for overhangs, internal and external bridges. Setting the fan speed specifically for these features can improve overall print quality and reduce warping.

parameters.label: Overhangs and external bridges fan speed
parameters.description: Use this part cooling fan speed when printing bridges or overhang walls with an overhang threshold that exceeds the value set in the 'Overhangs cooling threshold' parameter above. Increasing the cooling specifically for overhangs and bridges can improve the overall print quality of these features. Please note, this fan speed is clamped on the lower end by the minimum fan speed threshold set above. It is also adjusted upwards up to the maximum fan speed threshold when the minimum layer time threshold is not met.

parameters.label: Overhang cooling activation threshold
parameters.description: When the overhang exceeds this specified threshold, force the cooling fan to run at the 'Overhang Fan Speed' set below. This threshold is expressed as a percentage, indicating the portion of each line's width that is unsupported by the layer beneath it. Setting this value to 0% forces the cooling fan to run for all outer walls, regardless of the overhang degree.

parameters.label: External bridge infill direction
parameters.description: Bridging angle override. If left to zero, the bridging angle will be calculated automatically. Otherwise the provided angle will be used for external bridges. Use 180°for zero angle.

parameters.label: Internal bridge infill direction
parameters.description: Internal bridging angle override. If left to zero, the bridging angle will be calculated automatically. Otherwise the provided angle will be used for internal bridges. Use 180°for zero angle.  It is recommended to leave it at 0 unless there is a specific model need not to.

parameters.label: External bridge density
parameters.description: Controls the density (spacing) of external bridge lines. 100% means solid bridge. Default is 100%. Lower density external bridges can help improve reliability as there is more space for air to circulate around the extruded bridge, improving its cooling speed.

parameters.label: Internal bridge density
parameters.description: Controls the density (spacing) of internal bridge lines. 100% means solid bridge. Default is 100%. Lower density internal bridges can help reduce top surface pillowing and improve internal bridge reliability as there is more space for air to circulate around the extruded bridge, improving its cooling speed. This option works particularly well when combined with the second internal bridge over infill option, further improving internal bridging structure before solid infill is extruded.

parameters.label: Bridge flow ratio
parameters.description: Decrease this value slightly(for example 0.9) to reduce the amount of material for bridge, to improve sag.   The actual bridge flow used is calculated by multiplying this value with the filament flow ratio, and if set, the object's flow ratio.

parameters.label: Internal bridge flow ratio
parameters.description: This value governs the thickness of the internal bridge layer. This is the first layer over sparse infill. Decrease this value slightly (for example 0.9) to improve surface quality over sparse infill. The actual internal bridge flow used is calculated by multiplying this value with the bridge flow ratio, the filament flow ratio, and if set, the object's flow ratio.

parameters.label: Top surface flow ratio
parameters.description: This factor affects the amount of material for top solid infill. You can decrease it slightly to have smooth surface finish. The actual top surface flow used is calculated by multiplying this value with the filament flow ratio, and if set, the object's flow ratio.

parameters.label: Bottom surface flow ratio
parameters.description: This factor affects the amount of material for bottom solid infill. The actual bottom solid infill flow used is calculated by multiplying this value with the filament flow ratio, and if set, the object's flow ratio.

parameters.label: Precise wall
parameters.description: Improve shell precision by adjusting outer wall spacing. This also improves layer consistency.

parameters.label: Only one wall on top surfaces
parameters.description: Use only one wall on flat top surface, to give more space to the top infill pattern

parameters.label: One wall threshold
parameters.description: If a top surface has to be printed and it's partially covered by another layer, it won't be considered at a top layer where its width is below this value. This can be useful to not let the 'one perimeter on top' trigger on surface that should be covered only by perimeters. This value can be a mm or a % of the perimeter extrusion width. Warning: If enabled, artifacts can be created if you have some thin features on the next layer, like letters. Set this setting to 0 to remove these artifacts.

parameters.label: Only one wall on first layer
parameters.description: Use only one wall on first layer, to give more space to the bottom infill pattern

parameters.label: Extra perimeters on overhangs
parameters.description: Create additional perimeter paths over steep overhangs and areas where bridges cannot be anchored.

parameters.label: Reverse on even
parameters.description: Extrude perimeters that have a part over an overhang in the reverse direction on even layers. This alternating pattern can drastically improve steep overhangs.  This setting can also help reduce part warping due to the reduction of stresses in the part walls.

parameters.label: Reverse only internal perimeters
parameters.description: Apply the reverse perimeters logic only on internal perimeters.   This setting greatly reduces part stresses as they are now distributed in alternating directions. This should reduce part warping while also maintaining external wall quality. This feature can be very useful for warp prone material, like ABS/ASA, and also for elastic filaments, like TPU and Silk PLA. It can also help reduce warping on floating regions over supports.  For this setting to be the most effective, it is recommended to set the Reverse Threshold to 0 so that all internal walls print in alternating directions on even layers irrespective of their overhang degree.

parameters.label: Bridge counterbore holes
parameters.description: This option creates bridges for counterbore holes, allowing them to be printed without support. Available modes include: 1. None: No bridge is created. 2. Partially Bridged: Only a part of the unsupported area will be bridged. 3. Sacrificial Layer: A full sacrificial bridge layer is created.

parameters.label: Reverse threshold
parameters.description: Number of mm the overhang need to be for the reversal to be considered useful. Can be a % of the perimeter width. Value 0 enables reversal on every even layers regardless. When Detect overhang wall is not enabled, this option is ignored and reversal happens on every even layers regardless.

parameters.label: Classic mode
parameters.description: Enable this option to use classic mode

parameters.label: Slow down for overhang
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow printing down for different overhang degree

parameters.label: Slow down for curled perimeters
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow down printing in areas where perimeters may have curled upwards. For example, additional slowdown will be applied when printing overhangs on sharp corners like the front of the Benchy hull, reducing curling which compounds over multiple layers. It is generally recommended to have this option switched on unless your printer cooling is powerful enough or the print speed slow enough that perimeter curling does not happen. If printing with a high external perimeter speed, this parameter may introduce slight artifacts when slowing down due to the large variance in print speeds. If you notice artifacts, ensure your pressure advance is tuned correctly. Note: When this option is enabled, overhang perimeters are treated like overhangs, meaning the overhang speed is applied even if the overhanging perimeter is part of a bridge. For example, when the perimeters are 100% overhanging , with no wall supporting them from underneath, the 100% overhang speed will be applied.
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 10% and 25% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 25% and 50% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 50% and 75% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 75% and 100% line width. 0 means using original wall speed

parameters.label: External
parameters.description: Speed of the externally visible bridge extrusions. In addition, if Slow down for curled perimeters is disabled or Classic overhang mode is enabled, it will be the print speed of overhang walls that are supported by less than 13%, whether they are part of a bridge or an overhang.

parameters.label: Internal
parameters.description: Speed of internal bridges. If the value is expressed as a percentage, it will be calculated based on the bridge_speed. Default value is 150%.

parameters.label: Brim width
parameters.description: Distance from model to the outermost brim line

parameters.label: Brim type
parameters.description: This controls the generation of the brim at outer and/or inner side of models. Auto means the brim width is analyzed and calculated automatically.

parameters.label: Brim-object gap
parameters.description: A gap between innermost brim line and object can make brim be removed more easily

parameters.label: Brim ears
parameters.description: Only draw brim over the sharp edges of the model.

parameters.label: Brim ear max angle
parameters.description: Maximum angle to let a brim ear appear.  If set to 0, no brim will be created.  If set to ~180, brim will be created on everything but straight sections.

parameters.label: Brim ear detection radius
parameters.description: The geometry will be decimated before detecting sharp angles. This parameter indicates the minimum length of the deviation for the decimation. 0 to deactivate

parameters.label: Compatible machine condition
parameters.description: A boolean expression using the configuration values of an active printer profile. If this expression evaluates to true, this profile is considered compatible with the active printer profile.

parameters.label: Compatible process profiles condition
parameters.description: A boolean expression using the configuration values of an active print profile. If this expression evaluates to true, this profile is considered compatible with the active print profile.

parameters.label: Print sequence
parameters.description: Print sequence, layer by layer or object by object

parameters.label: Intra-layer order
parameters.description: Print order within a single layer

parameters.label: Slow printing down for better layer cooling
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow printing speed down to make the final layer time not shorter than the layer time threshold in \ , so that layer can be cooled for longer time. This can improve the cooling quality for needle and small details

parameters.label: Normal printing
parameters.description: The default acceleration of both normal printing and travel except initial layer

parameters.label: Default filament profile
parameters.description: Default filament profile when switch to this machine profile

parameters.label: Default process profile
parameters.description: Default process profile when switch to this machine profile

parameters.label: Activate air filtration
parameters.description: Activate for better air filtration. G-code command: M106 P3 S(0-255)

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of exhaust fan during printing.This speed will overwrite the speed in filament custom gcode

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of exhaust fan after printing completes

parameters.label: No cooling for the first
parameters.description: Close all cooling fan for the first certain layers. Cooling fan of the first layer used to be closed to get better build plate adhesion

parameters.label: Don't support bridges
parameters.description: Don't support the whole bridge area which make support very large. Bridge usually can be printing directly without support if not very long

parameters.label: Thick external bridges
parameters.description: If enabled, bridges are more reliable, can bridge longer distances, but may look worse. If disabled, bridges look better but are reliable just for shorter bridged distances.

parameters.label: Thick internal bridges
parameters.description: If enabled, thick internal bridges will be used. It's usually recommended to have this feature turned on. However, consider turning it off if you are using large nozzles.

parameters.label: Extra bridge layers (beta)
parameters.description: This option enables the generation of an extra bridge layer over internal and/or external bridges. Extra bridge layers help improve bridge appearance and reliability, as the solid infill is better supported. This is especially useful in fast printers, where the bridge and solid infill speeds vary greatly. The extra bridge layer results in reduced pillowing on top surfaces, as well as reduced separation of the external bridge layer from its surrounding perimeters. It is generally recommended to set this to at least 'External bridge only', unless specific issues with the sliced model are found. Options: 1. Disabled - does not generate second bridge layers. This is the default and is set for compatibility purposes. 2. External bridge only - generates second bridge layers for external-facing bridges only. Please note that small bridges that are shorter or narrower than the set number of perimeters will be skipped as they would not benefit from a second bridge layer. If generated, the second bridge layer will be extruded parallel to the first bridge layer to reinforce the bridge strength. 3. Internal bridge only - generates second bridge layers for internal bridges over sparse infill only. Please note that the internal bridges count towards the top shell layer count of your model. The second internal bridge layer will be extruded as close to perpendicular to the first as possible. If multiple regions in the same island, with varying bridge angles are present, the last region of that island will be selected as the angle reference. 4. Apply to all - generates second bridge layers for both internal and external-facing bridges

parameters.label: Filter out small internal bridges
parameters.description: This option can help reduce pillowing on top surfaces in heavily slanted or curved models. By default, small internal bridges are filtered out and the internal solid infill is printed directly over the sparse infill. This works well in most cases, speeding up printing without too much compromise on top surface quality.   However, in heavily slanted or curved models, especially where too low a sparse infill density is used, this may result in curling of the unsupported solid infill, causing pillowing. Enabling limited filtering or no filtering will print internal bridge layer over slightly unsupported internal solid infill. The options below control the sensitivity of the filtering, i.e. they control where internal bridges are created. 1. Filter - enables this option. This is the default behavior and works well in most cases. 2. Limited filtering - creates internal bridges on heavily slanted surfaces while avoiding unnecessary bridges. This works well for most difficult models. 3. No filtering - creates internal bridges on every potential internal overhang. This option is useful for

parameters.label: Max bridge length
parameters.description: Max length of bridges that don't need support. Set it to 0 if you want all bridges to be supported, and set it to a very large value if you don't want any bridges to be supported.

parameters.label: End G-code
parameters.description: End G-code when finish the whole printing
parameters.default: M104 S0 ; turn off temperature G28 X0  ; home X axis M84     ; disable motors

parameters.label: Between Object Gcode
parameters.description: Insert Gcode between objects. This parameter will only come into effect when you print your models object by object

parameters.label: End G-code
parameters.description: End G-code when finish the printing of this filament

parameters.label: Ensure vertical shell thickness
parameters.description: Add solid infill near sloping surfaces to guarantee the vertical shell thickness (top+bottom solid layers) None: No solid infill will be added anywhere. Caution: Use this option carefully if your model has sloped surfaces Critical Only: Avoid adding solid infill for walls Moderate: Add solid infill for heavily sloping surfaces only All: Add solid infill for all suitable sloping surfaces Default value is All.

parameters.label: Top surface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of top surface infill

parameters.label: Bottom surface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of bottom surface infill, not bridge infill

parameters.label: Internal solid infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of internal solid infill. if the detect narrow internal solid infill be enabled, the concentric pattern will be used for the small area.

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Line width of outer wall. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Speed of outer wall which is outermost and visible. It's used to be slower than inner wall speed to get better quality.

parameters.label: Small perimeters
parameters.description: This separate setting will affect the speed of perimeters having radius <= small_perimeter_threshold (usually holes). If expressed as percentage (for example: 80%) it will be calculated on the outer wall speed setting above. Set to zero for auto.

parameters.label: Small perimeters threshold
parameters.description: This sets the threshold for small perimeter length. Default threshold is 0mm

parameters.label: Walls printing order
parameters.description: Print sequence of the internal (inner) and external (outer) walls.   Use Inner/Outer for best overhangs. This is because the overhanging walls can adhere to a neighbouring perimeter while printing. However, this option results in slightly reduced surface quality as the external perimeter is deformed by being squashed to the internal perimeter.  Use Inner/Outer/Inner for the best external surface finish and dimensional accuracy as the external wall is printed undisturbed from an internal perimeter. However, overhang performance will reduce as there is no internal perimeter to print the external wall against. This option requires a minimum of 3 walls to be effective as it prints the internal walls from the 3rd perimeter onwards first, then the external perimeter and, finally, the first internal perimeter. This option is recommended against the Outer/Inner option in most cases.   Use Outer/Inner for the same external wall quality and dimensional accuracy benefits of Inner/Outer/Inner option. However, the z seams will appear less consistent as the first extrusion of a new layer starts on a visible surface.

parameters.label: Print infill first
parameters.description: Order of wall/infill. When the tickbox is unchecked the walls are printed first, which works best in most cases.  Printing infill first may help with extreme overhangs as the walls have the neighbouring infill to adhere to. However, the infill will slightly push out the printed walls where it is attached to them, resulting in a worse external surface finish. It can also cause the infill to shine through the external surfaces of the part.

parameters.label: Wall loop direction
parameters.description: The direction which the wall loops are extruded when looking down from the top.  By default all walls are extruded in counter-clockwise, unless Reverse on even is enabled. Set this to any option other than Auto will force the wall direction regardless of the Reverse on even.  This option will be disabled if spiral vase mode is enabled.

parameters.label: Extruder
parameters.description: The extruder to use (unless more specific extruder settings are specified). This value overrides perimeter and infill extruders, but not the support extruders.

parameters.label: Height to rod
parameters.description: Distance of the nozzle tip to the lower rod. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Height to lid
parameters.description: Distance of the nozzle tip to the lid. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description: Clearance radius around extruder. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Nozzle height
parameters.description: The height of nozzle tip.

parameters.label: Bed mesh min
parameters.description: This option sets the min point for the allowed bed mesh area. Due to the probe's XY offset, most printers are unable to probe the entire bed. To ensure the probe point does not go outside the bed area, the minimum and maximum points of the bed mesh should be set appropriately. OrcaSlicer ensures that adaptive_bed_mesh_min/adaptive_bed_mesh_max values do not exceed these min/max points. This information can usually be obtained from your printer manufacturer. The default setting is (-99999, -99999), which means there are no limits, thus allowing probing across the entire bed.

parameters.label: Bed mesh max
parameters.description: This option sets the max point for the allowed bed mesh area. Due to the probe's XY offset, most printers are unable to probe the entire bed. To ensure the probe point does not go outside the bed area, the minimum and maximum points of the bed mesh should be set appropriately. OrcaSlicer ensures that adaptive_bed_mesh_min/adaptive_bed_mesh_max values do not exceed these min/max points. This information can usually be obtained from your printer manufacturer. The default setting is (99999, 99999), which means there are no limits, thus allowing probing across the entire bed.

parameters.label: Probe point distance
parameters.description: This option sets the preferred distance between probe points (grid size) for the X and Y directions, with the default being 50mm for both X and Y.

parameters.label: Mesh margin
parameters.description: This option determines the additional distance by which the adaptive bed mesh area should be expanded in the XY directions.

parameters.label: Extruder Color
parameters.description: Only used as a visual help on UI

parameters.label: Extruder offset
parameters.description: If your firmware doesn't handle the extruder displacement you need the G-code to take it into account. This option lets you specify the displacement of each extruder with respect to the first one. It expects positive coordinates (they will be subtracted from the XY coordinate).

parameters.label: Flow ratio
parameters.description: The material may have volumetric change after switching between molten state and crystalline state. This setting changes all extrusion flow of this filament in gcode proportionally. Recommended value range is between 0.95 and 1.05. Maybe you can tune this value to get nice flat surface when there has slight overflow or underflow

parameters.label: Flow ratio
parameters.description: The material may have volumetric change after switching between molten state and crystalline state. This setting changes all extrusion flow of this filament in gcode proportionally. Recommended value range is between 0.95 and 1.05. Maybe you can tune this value to get nice flat surface when there has slight overflow or underflow. The final object flow ratio is this value multiplied by the filament flow ratio.

parameters.label: Enable pressure advance
parameters.description: Enable pressure advance, auto calibration result will be overwritten once enabled.

parameters.label: Pressure advance
parameters.description: Pressure advance(Klipper) AKA Linear advance factor(Marlin)

parameters.label: Enable adaptive pressure advance (beta)
parameters.description: With increasing print speeds (and hence increasing volumetric flow through the nozzle) and increasing accelerations, it has been observed that the effective PA value typically decreases. This means that a single PA value is not always 100% optimal for all features and a compromise value is usually used that does not cause too much bulging on features with lower flow speed and accelerations while also not causing gaps on faster features. This feature aims to address this limitation by modeling the response of your printer's extrusion system depending on the volumetric flow speed and acceleration it is printing at. Internally, it generates a fitted model that can extrapolate the needed pressure advance for any given volumetric flow speed and acceleration, which is then emitted to the printer depending on the current print conditions. When enabled, the pressure advance value above is overridden. However, a reasonable default value above is strongly recommended to act as a fallback and for when tool changing.

parameters.label: Adaptive pressure advance measurements (beta)
parameters.description: Add sets of pressure advance (PA) values, the volumetric flow speeds and accelerations they were measured at, separated by a comma. One set of values per line. For example 0.04,3.96,3000 0.033,3.96,10000 0.029,7.91,3000 0.026,7.91,10000 How to calibrate: 1. Run the pressure advance test for at least 3 speeds per acceleration value. It is recommended that the test is run for at least the speed of the external perimeters, the speed of the internal perimeters and the fastest feature print speed in your profile (usually its the sparse or solid infill). Then run them for the same speeds for the slowest and fastest print accelerations, and no faster than the recommended maximum acceleration as given by the Klipper input shaper. 2. Take note of the optimal PA value for each volumetric flow speed and acceleration. You can find the flow number by selecting flow from the color scheme drop down and move the horizontal slider over the PA pattern lines. The number should be visible at the bottom of the page. The ideal PA value should be decreasing the higher the volumetric flow is. If it is not, confirm that your extruder is functioning correctly. The slower and with less acceleration you print, the larger the range of acceptable PA values. If no difference is visible, use the PA value from the faster test. 3. Enter the triplets of PA values, Flow and Accelerations in the text box here and save your filament profile

parameters.label: Enable adaptive pressure advance for overhangs (beta)
parameters.description: Enable adaptive PA for overhangs as well as when flow changes within the same feature. This is an experimental option, as if the PA profile is not set accurately, it will cause uniformity issues on the external surfaces before and after overhangs.

parameters.label: Pressure advance for bridges
parameters.description: Pressure advance value for bridges. Set to 0 to disable.    A lower PA value when printing bridges helps reduce the appearance of slight under extrusion immediately after bridges. This is caused by the pressure drop in the nozzle when printing in the air and a lower PA helps counteract this.

parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: Default line width if other line widths are set to 0. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Keep fan always on
parameters.description: If enable this setting, part cooling fan will never be stopped and will run at least at minimum speed to reduce the frequency of starting and stopping

parameters.label: Don't slow down outer walls
parameters.description: If enabled, this setting will ensure external perimeters are not slowed down to meet the minimum layer time. This is particularly helpful in the below scenarios: 1. To avoid changes in shine when printing glossy filaments 2. To avoid changes in external wall speed which may create slight wall artifacts that appear like z banding 3. To avoid printing at speeds which cause VFAs (fine artifacts) on the external walls

parameters.label: Layer time
parameters.description: Part cooling fan will be enabled for layers of which estimated time is shorter than this value. Fan speed is interpolated between the minimum and maximum fan speeds according to layer printing time

parameters.label: Default color
parameters.description: Default filament color

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description: Only used as a visual help on UI

parameters.label: Filament notes
parameters.description: You can put your notes regarding the filament here.

parameters.label: Required nozzle HRC
parameters.description: Minimum HRC of nozzle required to print the filament. Zero means no checking of nozzle's HRC.

parameters.label: Max volumetric speed
parameters.description: This setting stands for how much volume of filament can be melted and extruded per second. Printing speed is limited by max volumetric speed, in case of too high and unreasonable speed setting. Can't be zero

parameters.label: Filament load time
parameters.description: Time to load new filament when switch filament. It's usually applicable for single-extruder multi-material machines. For tool changers or multi-tool machines, it's typically 0. For statistics only

parameters.label: Filament unload time
parameters.description: Time to unload old filament when switch filament. It's usually applicable for single-extruder multi-material machines. For tool changers or multi-tool machines, it's typically 0. For statistics only

parameters.label: Tool change time
parameters.description: Time taken to switch tools. It's usually applicable for tool changers or multi-tool machines. For single-extruder multi-material machines, it's typically 0. For statistics only

parameters.label: Diameter
parameters.description: Filament diameter is used to calculate extrusion in gcode, so it's important and should be accurate

parameters.label: Pellet flow coefficient
parameters.description: Pellet flow coefficient is empirically derived and allows for volume calculation for pellet printers.  Internally it is converted to filament_diameter. All other volume calculations remain the same.  filament_diameter = sqrt( (4 * pellet_flow_coefficient) / PI )

parameters.label: Shrinkage (XY)
parameters.description: Enter the shrinkage percentage that the filament will get after cooling (94% if you measure 94mm instead of 100mm). The part will be scaled in xy to compensate. Only the filament used for the perimeter is taken into account. Be sure to allow enough space between objects, as this compensation is done after the checks.

parameters.label: Shrinkage (Z)
parameters.description: Enter the shrinkage percentage that the filament will get after cooling (94% if you measure 94mm instead of 100mm). The part will be scaled in Z to compensate.

parameters.label: Loading speed
parameters.description: Speed used for loading the filament on the wipe tower.

parameters.label: Loading speed at the start
parameters.description: Speed used at the very beginning of loading phase.

parameters.label: Unloading speed
parameters.description: Speed used for unloading the filament on the wipe tower (does not affect initial part of unloading just after ramming).

parameters.label: Unloading speed at the start
parameters.description: Speed used for unloading the tip of the filament immediately after ramming.

parameters.label: Delay after unloading
parameters.description: Time to wait after the filament is unloaded. May help to get reliable toolchanges with flexible materials that may need more time to shrink to original dimensions.

parameters.label: Number of cooling moves
parameters.description: Filament is cooled by being moved back and forth in the cooling tubes. Specify desired number of these moves.

parameters.label: Stamping loading speed
parameters.description: Speed used for stamping.

parameters.label: Stamping distance measured from the center of the cooling tube
parameters.description: If set to nonzero value, filament is moved toward the nozzle between the individual cooling moves (\ ). This option configures how long this movement should be before the filament is retracted again.

parameters.label: Speed of the first cooling move
parameters.description: Cooling moves are gradually accelerating beginning at this speed.

parameters.label: Minimal purge on wipe tower
parameters.description: After a tool change, the exact position of the newly loaded filament inside the nozzle may not be known, and the filament pressure is likely not yet stable. Before purging the print head into an infill or a sacrificial object, Orca Slicer will always prime this amount of material into the wipe tower to produce successive infill or sacrificial object extrusions reliably.

parameters.label: Speed of the last cooling move
parameters.description: Cooling moves are gradually accelerating towards this speed.

parameters.label: Ramming parameters
parameters.description: This string is edited by RammingDialog and contains ramming specific parameters.

parameters.label: Enable ramming for multi-tool setups
parameters.description: Perform ramming when using multi-tool printer (i.e. when the 'Single Extruder Multimaterial' in Printer Settings is unchecked). When checked, a small amount of filament is rapidly extruded on the wipe tower just before the toolchange. This option is only used when the wipe tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Multi-tool ramming volume
parameters.description: The volume to be rammed before the toolchange.

parameters.label: Multi-tool ramming flow
parameters.description: Flow used for ramming the filament before the toolchange.

parameters.label: Density
parameters.description: Filament density. For statistics only

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The material type of filament

parameters.label: Soluble material
parameters.description: Soluble material is commonly used to print support and support interface

parameters.label: Support material
parameters.description: Support material is commonly used to print support and support interface

parameters.label: Softening temperature
parameters.description: The material softens at this temperature, so when the bed temperature is equal to or greater than it, it's highly recommended to open the front door and/or remove the upper glass to avoid clogging.

parameters.label: Price
parameters.description: Filament price. For statistics only

parameters.label: Vendor
parameters.description: Vendor of filament. For show only

parameters.label: Sparse infill direction
parameters.description: Angle for sparse infill pattern, which controls the start or main direction of line

parameters.label: Solid infill direction
parameters.description: Angle for solid infill pattern, which controls the start or main direction of line

parameters.label: Rotate solid infill direction
parameters.description: Rotate the solid infill direction by 90° for each layer.

parameters.label: Sparse infill density
parameters.description: Density of internal sparse infill, 100% turns all sparse infill into solid infill and internal solid infill pattern will be used

parameters.label: Sparse infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern for internal sparse infill

parameters.label: Lattice angle 1
parameters.description: The angle of the first set of 2D lattice elements in the Z direction. Zero is vertical.

parameters.label: Lattice angle 2
parameters.description: The angle of the second set of 2D lattice elements in the Z direction. Zero is vertical.

parameters.label: Sparse infill anchor length
parameters.description: Connect an infill line to an internal perimeter with a short segment of an additional perimeter. If expressed as percentage (example: 15%) it is calculated over infill extrusion width. Orca Slicer tries to connect two close infill lines to a short perimeter segment. If no such perimeter segment shorter than infill_anchor_max is found, the infill line is connected to a perimeter segment at just one side and the length of the perimeter segment taken is limited to this parameter, but no longer than anchor_length_max. Set this parameter to zero to disable anchoring perimeters connected to a single infill line.

parameters.label: Maximum length of the infill anchor
parameters.description: Connect an infill line to an internal perimeter with a short segment of an additional perimeter. If expressed as percentage (example: 15%) it is calculated over infill extrusion width. Orca Slicer tries to connect two close infill lines to a short perimeter segment. If no such perimeter segment shorter than this parameter is found, the infill line is connected to a perimeter segment at just one side and the length of the perimeter segment taken is limited to infill_anchor, but no longer than this parameter. If set to 0, the old algorithm for infill connection will be used, it should create the same result as with 1000 & 0.

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Acceleration of inner walls

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: Acceleration of travel moves

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Acceleration of top surface infill. Using a lower value may improve top surface quality

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Acceleration of outer wall. Using a lower value can improve quality

parameters.label: Bridge
parameters.description: Acceleration of bridges. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 50%), it will be calculated based on the outer wall acceleration.

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.description: Acceleration of sparse infill. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 100%), it will be calculated based on the default acceleration.

parameters.label: Internal solid infill
parameters.description: Acceleration of internal solid infill. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 100%), it will be calculated based on the default acceleration.

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Acceleration of initial layer. Using a lower value can improve build plate adhesive

parameters.label: Enable accel_to_decel
parameters.description: Klipper's max_accel_to_decel will be adjusted automatically

parameters.label: accel_to_decel
parameters.description: Klipper's max_accel_to_decel will be adjusted to this %% of acceleration

parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: Default

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Jerk of outer walls

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Jerk of inner walls

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Jerk for top surface

parameters.label: Infill
parameters.description: Jerk for infill

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Jerk for initial layer

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: Jerk for travel

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Line width of initial layer. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Initial layer height
parameters.description: Height of initial layer. Making initial layer height to be thick slightly can improve build plate adhesion

parameters.label: Adaptive layer height
parameters.description: Enabling this option means the height of every layer except the first will be automatically calculated during slicing according to the slope of the model’s surface. Note that this option only takes effect if no prime tower is generated in current plate.

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Speed of initial layer except the solid infill part

parameters.label: Initial layer infill
parameters.description: Speed of solid infill part of initial layer

parameters.label: Initial layer travel speed
parameters.description: Travel speed of initial layer

parameters.label: Number of slow layers
parameters.description: The first few layers are printed slower than normal. The speed is gradually increased in a linear fashion over the specified number of layers.

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Nozzle temperature to print initial layer when using this filament

parameters.label: Full fan speed at layer
parameters.description: Fan speed will be ramped up linearly from zero at layer \  to maximum at layer \ . \ will be ignored if lower than \ , in which case the fan will be running at maximum allowed speed at layer \ + 1.

parameters.label: Support interface fan speed
parameters.description: This part cooling fan speed is applied when printing support interfaces. Setting this parameter to a higher than regular speed reduces the layer binding strength between supports and the supported part, making them easier to separate. Set to -1 to disable it. This setting is overridden by disable_fan_first_layers.

parameters.label: Internal bridges fan speed
parameters.description: The part cooling fan speed used for all internal bridges. Set to -1 to use the overhang fan speed settings instead. Reducing the internal bridges fan speed, compared to your regular fan speed, can help reduce part warping due to excessive cooling applied over a large surface for a prolonged period of time.

parameters.label: Fuzzy Skin
parameters.description: Randomly jitter while printing the wall, so that the surface has a rough look. This setting controls the fuzzy position

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin thickness
parameters.description: The width within which to jitter. It's advised to be below outer wall line width

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin point distance
parameters.description: The average distance between the random points introduced on each line segment

parameters.label: Apply fuzzy skin to first layer
parameters.description: Whether to apply fuzzy skin on the first layer

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin noise type
parameters.description: Noise type to use for fuzzy skin generation. Classic: Classic uniform random noise. Perlin: Perlin noise, which gives a more consistent texture. Billow: Similar to perlin noise, but clumpier. Ridged Multifractal: Ridged noise with sharp, jagged features. Creates marble-like textures. Voronoi: Divides the surface into voronoi cells, and displaces each one by a random amount. Creates a patchwork texture.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin feature size
parameters.description: The base size of the coherent noise features, in mm. Higher values will result in larger features.

parameters.label: Fuzzy Skin Noise Octaves
parameters.description: The number of octaves of coherent noise to use. Higher values increase the detail of the noise, but also increase computation time.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin noise persistence
parameters.description: The decay rate for higher octaves of the coherent noise. Lower values will result in smoother noise.

parameters.label: Filter out tiny gaps
parameters.description: Don't print gap fill with a length is smaller than the threshold specified (in mm). This setting applies to top, bottom and solid infill and, if using the classic perimeter generator, to wall gap fill.

parameters.label: Gap infill
parameters.description: Speed of gap infill. Gap usually has irregular line width and should be printed more slowly

parameters.label: Precise Z height
parameters.description: Enable this to get precise z height of object after slicing. It will get the precise object height by fine-tuning the layer heights of the last few layers. Note that this is an experimental parameter.

parameters.label: Arc fitting
parameters.description: Enable this to get a G-code file which has G2 and G3 moves. The fitting tolerance is same as the resolution. Note: For Klipper machines, this option is recommended to be disabled. Klipper does not benefit from arc commands as these are split again into line segments by the firmware. This results in a reduction in surface quality as line segments are converted to arcs by the slicer and then back to line segments by the firmware.

parameters.label: Add line number
parameters.description: Enable this to add line number(Nx) at the beginning of each G-Code line

parameters.label: Scan first layer
parameters.description: Enable this to enable the camera on printer to check the quality of first layer

parameters.label: Enable spaghetti detector
parameters.description: Enable the camera on printer to check spaghetti

parameters.label: Nozzle type
parameters.description: The metallic material of nozzle. This determines the abrasive resistance of nozzle, and what kind of filament can be printed

parameters.label: Nozzle HRC
parameters.description: The nozzle's hardness. Zero means no checking for nozzle's hardness during slicing.

parameters.label: Printer structure
parameters.description: The physical arrangement and components of a printing device

parameters.label: Best object position
parameters.description: Best auto arranging position in range [0,1] w.r.t. bed shape.

parameters.label: Auxiliary part cooling fan
parameters.description: Enable this option if machine has auxiliary part cooling fan. G-code command: M106 P2 S(0-255).

parameters.label: Fan speed-up time
parameters.description: Start the fan this number of seconds earlier than its target start time (you can use fractional seconds). It assumes infinite acceleration for this time estimation, and will only take into account G1 and G0 moves (arc fitting is unsupported). It won't move fan commands from custom gcodes (they act as a sort of 'barrier'). It won't move fan commands into the start gcode if the 'only custom start gcode' is activated. Use 0 to deactivate.

parameters.label: Only overhangs
parameters.description: Will only take into account the delay for the cooling of overhangs.

parameters.label: Fan kick-start time
parameters.description: Emit a max fan speed command for this amount of seconds before reducing to target speed to kick-start the cooling fan. This is useful for fans where a low PWM/power may be insufficient to get the fan started spinning from a stop, or to get the fan up to speed faster. Set to 0 to deactivate.

parameters.label: Time cost
parameters.description: The printer cost per hour

parameters.label: Support control chamber temperature
parameters.description: This option is enabled if machine support controlling chamber temperature G-code command: M141 S(0-255)

parameters.label: Support air filtration
parameters.description: Enable this if printer support air filtration G-code command: M106 P3 S(0-255)

parameters.label: G-code flavor
parameters.description: What kind of gcode the printer is compatible with

parameters.label: Pellet Modded Printer
parameters.description: Enable this option if your printer uses pellets instead of filaments

parameters.label: Support multi bed types
parameters.description: Enable this option if you want to use multiple bed types

parameters.label: Label objects
parameters.description: Enable this to add comments into the G-Code labeling print moves with what object they belong to, which is useful for the Octoprint CancelObject plugin. This settings is NOT compatible with Single Extruder Multi Material setup and Wipe into Object / Wipe into Infill.

parameters.label: Exclude objects
parameters.description: Enable this option to add EXCLUDE OBJECT command in g-code

parameters.label: Verbose G-code
parameters.description: Enable this to get a commented G-code file, with each line explained by a descriptive text. If you print from SD card, the additional weight of the file could make your firmware slow down.

parameters.label: Infill combination
parameters.description: Automatically Combine sparse infill of several layers to print together to reduce time. Wall is still printed with original layer height.

parameters.label: Infill combination - Max layer height
parameters.description: Maximum layer height for the combined sparse infill.   Set it to 0 or 100% to use the nozzle diameter (for maximum reduction in print time) or a value of ~80% to maximize sparse infill strength. The number of layers over which infill is combined is derived by dividing this value with the layer height and rounded down to the nearest decimal. Use either absolute mm values (eg. 0.32mm for a 0.4mm nozzle) or % values (eg 80%). This value must not be larger than the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Infill
parameters.description: Filament to print internal sparse infill.

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.label_ja: スパースインフィルライン幅
parameters.description: Line width of internal sparse infill. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Infill/Wall overlap
parameters.description: Infill area is enlarged slightly to overlap with wall for better bonding. The percentage value is relative to line width of sparse infill. Set this value to ~10-15% to minimize potential over extrusion and accumulation of material resulting in rough top surfaces.

parameters.label: Top/Bottom solid infill/wall overlap
parameters.description: Top solid infill area is enlarged slightly to overlap with wall for better bonding and to minimize the appearance of pinholes where the top infill meets the walls. A value of 25-30% is a good starting point, minimizing the appearance of pinholes. The percentage value is relative to line width of sparse infill

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.description: Speed of internal sparse infill

parameters.label: Inherits profile
parameters.description: Name of parent profile

parameters.label: Interface shells
parameters.description: Force the generation of solid shells between adjacent materials/volumes. Useful for multi-extruder prints with translucent materials or manual soluble support material

parameters.label: Maximum width of a segmented region
parameters.description: Maximum width of a segmented region. Zero disables this feature.

parameters.label: Interlocking depth of a segmented region
parameters.description: Interlocking depth of a segmented region. It will be ignored if \ is zero or if \  is bigger then \ . Zero disables this feature.

parameters.label: Use beam interlocking
parameters.description: Generate interlocking beam structure at the locations where different filaments touch. This improves the adhesion between filaments, especially models printed in different materials.

parameters.label: Interlocking beam width
parameters.description: The width of the interlocking structure beams.

parameters.label: Interlocking direction
parameters.description: Orientation of interlock beams.

parameters.label: Interlocking beam layers
parameters.description: The height of the beams of the interlocking structure, measured in number of layers. Less layers is stronger, but more prone to defects.

parameters.label: Interlocking depth
parameters.description: The distance from the boundary between filaments to generate interlocking structure, measured in cells. Too few cells will result in poor adhesion.

parameters.label: Interlocking boundary avoidance
parameters.description: The distance from the outside of a model where interlocking structures will not be generated, measured in cells.

parameters.label: Ironing Type
parameters.description: Ironing is using small flow to print on same height of surface again to make flat surface more smooth. This setting controls which layer being ironed

parameters.label: Ironing Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern that will be used when ironing

parameters.label: Ironing flow
parameters.description: The amount of material to extrude during ironing. Relative to flow of normal layer height. Too high value results in overextrusion on the surface

parameters.label: Ironing line spacing
parameters.description: The distance between the lines of ironing

parameters.label: Ironing inset
parameters.description: The distance to keep from the edges. A value of 0 sets this to half of the nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Ironing speed
parameters.description: Print speed of ironing lines

parameters.label: Ironing angle
parameters.description: The angle ironing is done at. A negative number disables this function and uses the default method.

parameters.label: Layer change G-code
parameters.description: This gcode part is inserted at every layer change after lift z

parameters.label: Supports silent mode
parameters.description: Whether the machine supports silent mode in which machine use lower acceleration to print

parameters.label: Emit limits to G-code
parameters.description: If enabled, the machine limits will be emitted to G-code file. This option will be ignored if the g-code flavor is set to Klipper.

parameters.label: Pause G-code
parameters.description: This G-code will be used as a code for the pause print. User can insert pause G-code in gcode viewer

parameters.label: Custom G-code
parameters.description: This G-code will be used as a custom code

parameters.label: Small area flow compensation (beta)
parameters.description: Enable flow compensation for small infill areas

parameters.label: Flow Compensation Model
parameters.description: Flow Compensation Model, used to adjust the flow for small infill areas. The model is expressed as a comma separated pair of values for extrusion length and flow correction factors, one per line, in the following format: \

parameters.label: Maximum speed %1%
parameters.description: Maximum speed of %1% axis

parameters.label: Minimum speed for extruding
parameters.description: Minimum speed for extruding (M205 S)

parameters.label: Minimum travel speed
parameters.description: Minimum travel speed (M205 T)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for extruding
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for extruding (M204 P)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for retracting
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for retracting (M204 R)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for travel
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for travel (M204 T), it only applies to Marlin 2

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Part cooling fan speed may be increased when auto cooling is enabled. This is the maximum speed limitation of part cooling fan

parameters.label: Max
parameters.description: The largest printable layer height for extruder. Used tp limits the maximum layer hight when enable adaptive layer height

parameters.label: Extrusion rate smoothing
parameters.description: This parameter smooths out sudden extrusion rate changes that happen when the printer transitions from printing a high flow (high speed/larger width) extrusion to a lower flow (lower speed/smaller width) extrusion and vice versa. It defines the maximum rate by which the extruded volumetric flow in mm3/sec can change over time. Higher values mean higher extrusion rate changes are allowed, resulting in faster speed transitions. A value of 0 disables the feature. For a high speed, high flow direct drive printer (like the Bambu lab or Voron) this value is usually not needed. However it can provide some marginal benefit in certain cases where feature speeds vary greatly. For example, when there are aggressive slowdowns due to overhangs. In these cases a high value of around 300-350mm3/s2 is recommended as this allows for just enough smoothing to assist pressure advance achieve a smoother flow transition. For slower printers without pressure advance, the value should be set much lower. A value of 10-15mm3/s2 is a good starting point for direct drive extruders and 5-10mm3/s2 for Bowden style. This feature is known as Pressure Equalizer in Prusa slicer. Note: this parameter disables arc fitting.

parameters.label: Smoothing segment length
parameters.description: A lower value results in smoother extrusion rate transitions. However, this results in a significantly larger gcode file and more instructions for the printer to process. Default value of 3 works well for most cases. If your printer is stuttering, increase this value to reduce the number of adjustments made Allowed values: 0.5-5

parameters.label: Apply only on external features
parameters.description: Applies extrusion rate smoothing only on external perimeters and overhangs. This can help reduce artefacts due to sharp speed transitions on externally visible overhangs without impacting the print speed of features that will not be visible to the user.

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Minimum speed for part cooling fan

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of auxiliary part cooling fan. Auxiliary fan will run at this speed during printing except the first several layers which is defined by no cooling layers. Please enable auxiliary_fan in printer settings to use this feature. G-code command: M106 P2 S(0-255)

parameters.label: Min
parameters.description: The lowest printable layer height for extruder. Used tp limits the minimum layer hight when enable adaptive layer height

parameters.label: Min print speed
parameters.description: The minimum print speed to which the printer slows down to maintain the minimum layer time defined above when the slowdown for better layer cooling is enabled.

parameters.label: Nozzle diameter
parameters.description: Diameter of nozzle

parameters.label: Configuration notes
parameters.description: You can put here your personal notes. This text will be added to the G-code header comments.

parameters.label: Host Type
parameters.description: Orca Slicer can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field must contain the kind of the host.

parameters.label: Nozzle volume
parameters.description: Volume of nozzle between the cutter and the end of nozzle

parameters.label: Cooling tube position
parameters.description: Distance of the center-point of the cooling tube from the extruder tip.

parameters.label: Cooling tube length
parameters.description: Length of the cooling tube to limit space for cooling moves inside it.

parameters.label: High extruder current on filament swap
parameters.description: It may be beneficial to increase the extruder motor current during the filament exchange sequence to allow for rapid ramming feed rates and to overcome resistance when loading a filament with an ugly shaped tip.

parameters.label: Filament parking position
parameters.description: Distance of the extruder tip from the position where the filament is parked when unloaded. This should match the value in printer firmware.

parameters.label: Extra loading distance
parameters.description: When set to zero, the distance the filament is moved from parking position during load is exactly the same as it was moved back during unload. When positive, it is loaded further, if negative, the loading move is shorter than unloading.

parameters.label: Start end points
parameters.description: The start and end points which is from cutter area to garbage can.

parameters.label: Reduce infill retraction
parameters.description: Don't retract when the travel is in infill area absolutely. That means the oozing can't been seen. This can reduce times of retraction for complex model and save printing time, but make slicing and G-code generating slower

parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: This option will drop the temperature of the inactive extruders to prevent oozing.

parameters.label: Filename format
parameters.description: User can self-define the project file name when export

parameters.label: Make overhangs printable
parameters.description: Modify the geometry to print overhangs without support material.

parameters.label: Make overhangs printable - Maximum angle
parameters.description: Maximum angle of overhangs to allow after making more steep overhangs printable. 90° will not change the model at all and allow any overhang, while 0 will replace all overhangs with conical material.

parameters.label: Make overhangs printable - Hole area
parameters.description: Maximum area of a hole in the base of the model before it's filled by conical material. A value of 0 will fill all the holes in the model base.

parameters.label: Detect overhang wall
parameters.description: Detect the overhang percentage relative to line width and use different speed to print. For 100%% overhang, bridge speed is used.

parameters.label: Walls
parameters.description: Filament to print walls

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Line width of inner wall. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Speed of inner wall

parameters.label: Wall loops
parameters.description: Number of walls of every layer

parameters.label: Alternate extra wall
parameters.description: This setting adds an extra wall to every other layer. This way the infill gets wedged vertically between the walls, resulting in stronger prints.   When this option is enabled, the ensure vertical shell thickness option needs to be disabled.   Using lightning infill together with this option is not recommended as there is limited infill to anchor the extra perimeters to.

parameters.label: Post-processing Scripts
parameters.description: If you want to process the output G-code through custom scripts, just list their absolute paths here. Separate multiple scripts with a semicolon. Scripts will be passed the absolute path to the G-code file as the first argument, and they can access the Orca Slicer config settings by reading environment variables.

parameters.label: Printer type
parameters.description: Type of the printer

parameters.label: Printer notes
parameters.description: You can put your notes regarding the printer here.

parameters.label: Printer variant
parameters.description: Name of the printer variant. For example, the printer variants may be differentiated by a nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Raft contact Z distance
parameters.description: Z gap between object and raft. Ignored for soluble interface

parameters.label: Raft expansion
parameters.description: Expand all raft layers in XY plane

parameters.label: Initial layer density
parameters.description: Density of the first raft or support layer

parameters.label: Initial layer expansion
parameters.description: Expand the first raft or support layer to improve bed plate adhesion

parameters.label: Raft layers
parameters.description: Object will be raised by this number of support layers. Use this function to avoid wrapping when print ABS

parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description: G-code path is generated after simplifying the contour of model to avoid too much points and gcode lines in gcode file. Smaller value means higher resolution and more time to slice

parameters.label: Travel distance threshold
parameters.description: Only trigger retraction when the travel distance is longer than this threshold

parameters.label: Retract amount before wipe
parameters.description: The length of fast retraction before wipe, relative to retraction length

parameters.label: Retract when change layer
parameters.description: Force a retraction when changes layer

parameters.label: Retract on top layer
parameters.description: Force a retraction on top layer. Disabling could prevent clog on very slow patterns with small movements, like Hilbert curve

parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: Some amount of material in extruder is pulled back to avoid ooze during long travel. Set zero to disable retraction

parameters.label: Long retraction when cut(beta)
parameters.description: Experimental feature.Retracting and cutting off the filament at a longer distance during changes to minimize purge. While this reduces flush significantly, it may also raise the risk of nozzle clogs or other printing problems.

parameters.label: Retraction distance when cut
parameters.description: Experimental feature.Retraction length before cutting off during filament change

parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: When retraction is triggered before changing tool, filament is pulled back by the specified amount (the length is measured on raw filament, before it enters the extruder).

parameters.label: Z-hop height
parameters.description: Whenever the retraction is done, the nozzle is lifted a little to create clearance between nozzle and the print. It prevents nozzle from hitting the print when travel move. Using spiral line to lift z can prevent stringing

parameters.label: Z-hop type
parameters.description: Z hop type

parameters.label: Traveling angle
parameters.description: Traveling angle for Slope and Spiral Z hop type. Setting it to 90° results in Normal Lift

parameters.label: Only lift Z above
parameters.description: If you set this to a positive value, Z lift will only take place above the specified absolute Z.

parameters.label: Only lift Z below
parameters.description: If you set this to a positive value, Z lift will only take place below the specified absolute Z.

parameters.label: On surfaces
parameters.description: Enforce Z Hop behavior. This setting is impacted by the above settings (Only lift Z above/below).

parameters.label: Extra length on restart
parameters.description: When the retraction is compensated after the travel move, the extruder will push this additional amount of filament. This setting is rarely needed.

parameters.label: Extra length on restart
parameters.description: When the retraction is compensated after changing tool, the extruder will push this additional amount of filament.

parameters.label: Retraction Speed
parameters.description: Speed of retractions

parameters.label: De-retraction Speed
parameters.description: Speed for reloading filament into extruder. Zero means same speed with retraction

parameters.label: Use firmware retraction
parameters.description: This experimental setting uses G10 and G11 commands to have the firmware handle the retraction. This is only supported in recent Marlin.

parameters.label: Disable set remaining print time
parameters.description: Disable generating of the M73: Set remaining print time in the final gcode

parameters.label: Seam position
parameters.description: The start position to print each part of outer wall

parameters.label: Staggered inner seams
parameters.description: This option causes the inner seams to be shifted backwards based on their depth, forming a zigzag pattern.

parameters.label: Seam gap
parameters.description: In order to reduce the visibility of the seam in a closed loop extrusion, the loop is interrupted and shortened by a specified amount. This amount can be specified in millimeters or as a percentage of the current extruder diameter. The default value for this parameter is 10%.

parameters.label: Scarf joint seam (beta)
parameters.description: Use scarf joint to minimize seam visibility and increase seam strength.

parameters.label: Conditional scarf joint
parameters.description: Apply scarf joints only to smooth perimeters where traditional seams do not conceal the seams at sharp corners effectively.

parameters.label: Conditional angle threshold
parameters.description: This option sets the threshold angle for applying a conditional scarf joint seam. If the maximum angle within the perimeter loop exceeds this value (indicating the absence of sharp corners), a scarf joint seam will be used. The default value is 155°.

parameters.label: Conditional overhang threshold
parameters.description: This option determines the overhang threshold for the application of scarf joint seams. If the unsupported portion of the perimeter is less than this threshold, scarf joint seams will be applied. The default threshold is set at 40% of the external wall's width. Due to performance considerations, the degree of overhang is estimated.

parameters.label: Scarf joint speed
parameters.description: This option sets the printing speed for scarf joints. It is recommended to print scarf joints at a slow speed (less than 100 mm/s).  It's also advisable to enable 'Extrusion rate smoothing' if the set speed varies significantly from the speed of the outer or inner walls. If the speed specified here is higher than the speed of the outer or inner walls, the printer will default to the slower of the two speeds. When specified as a percentage (e.g., 80%), the speed is calculated based on the respective outer or inner wall speed. The default value is set to 100%.

parameters.label: Scarf joint flow ratio
parameters.description: This factor affects the amount of material for scarf joints.

parameters.label: Scarf start height
parameters.description: Start height of the scarf. This amount can be specified in millimeters or as a percentage of the current layer height. The default value for this parameter is 0.

parameters.label: Scarf around entire wall
parameters.description: The scarf extends to the entire length of the wall.

parameters.label: Scarf length
parameters.description: Length of the scarf. Setting this parameter to zero effectively disables the scarf.

parameters.label: Scarf steps
parameters.description: Minimum number of segments of each scarf.

parameters.label: Scarf joint for inner walls
parameters.description: Use scarf joint for inner walls as well.

parameters.label: Role base wipe speed
parameters.description: The wipe speed is determined by the speed of the current extrusion role. e.g. if a wipe action is executed immediately following an outer wall extrusion, the speed of the outer wall extrusion will be utilized for the wipe action.

parameters.label: Wipe on loops
parameters.description: To minimize the visibility of the seam in a closed loop extrusion, a small inward movement is executed before the extruder leaves the loop.

parameters.label: Wipe before external loop
parameters.description: To minimize visibility of potential overextrusion at the start of an external perimeter when printing with Outer/Inner or Inner/Outer/Inner wall print order, the de-retraction is performed slightly on the inside from the start of the external perimeter. That way any potential over extrusion is hidden from the outside surface.   This is useful when printing with Outer/Inner or Inner/Outer/Inner wall print order as in these modes it is more likely an external perimeter is printed immediately after a de-retraction move.

parameters.label: Wipe speed
parameters.description: The wipe speed is determined by the speed setting specified in this configuration. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 80%), it will be calculated based on the travel speed setting above. The default value for this parameter is 80%

parameters.label: Skirt distance
parameters.description: Distance from skirt to brim or object

parameters.label: Skirt start point
parameters.description: Angle from the object center to skirt start point. Zero is the most right position, counter clockwise is positive angle.

parameters.label: Skirt height
parameters.description: How many layers of skirt. Usually only one layer

parameters.label: Single loop draft shield
parameters.description: Limits the draft shield loops to one wall after the first layer. This is useful, on occasion, to conserve filament but may cause the draft shield to warp / crack.

parameters.label: Draft shield
parameters.description: A draft shield is useful to protect an ABS or ASA print from warping and detaching from print bed due to wind draft. It is usually needed only with open frame printers, i.e. without an enclosure. Enabled = skirt is as tall as the highest printed object. Otherwise 'Skirt height' is used. Note: With the draft shield active, the skirt will be printed at skirt distance from the object. Therefore, if brims are active it may intersect with them. To avoid this, increase the skirt distance value.

parameters.label: Skirt type
parameters.description: Combined - single skirt for all objects, Per object - individual object skirt.
