parameters.label: Skirt loops
parameters.description: Number of loops for the skirt. Zero means disabling skirt

parameters.label: Skirt speed
parameters.description: Speed of skirt, in mm/s. Zero means use default layer extrusion speed.

parameters.label: Skirt minimum extrusion length
parameters.description: Minimum filament extrusion length in mm when printing the skirt. Zero means this feature is disabled. Using a non zero value is useful if the printer is set up to print without a prime line. Final number of loops is not taling into account whli arranging or validating objects distance. Increase loop number in such case.

parameters.label: Layer time
parameters.description: The printing speed in exported gcode will be slowed down, when the estimated layer time is shorter than this value, to get better cooling for these layers

parameters.label: Minimum sparse infill threshold
parameters.description: Sparse infill area which is smaller than threshold value is replaced by internal solid infill

parameters.label: Solid infill
parameters.description: Filament to print solid infill

parameters.label: Internal solid infill
parameters.description: Line width of internal solid infill. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Internal solid infill
parameters.description: Speed of internal solid infill, not the top and bottom surface

parameters.label: Spiral vase
parameters.description: Spiralize smooths out the z moves of the outer contour. And turns a solid model into a single walled print with solid bottom layers. The final generated model has no seam

parameters.label: Smooth Spiral
parameters.description: Smooth Spiral smooths out X and Y moves as well, resulting in no visible seam at all, even in the XY directions on walls that are not vertical

parameters.label: Max XY Smoothing
parameters.description: Maximum distance to move points in XY to try to achieve a smooth spiral. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Spiral starting flow ratio
parameters.description: Sets the starting flow ratio while transitioning from the last bottom layer to the spiral. Normally the spiral transition scales the flow ratio from 0% to 100% during the first loop which can in some cases lead to under extrusion at the start of the spiral.

parameters.label: Spiral finishing flow ratio
parameters.description: Sets the finishing flow ratio while ending the spiral. Normally the spiral transition scales the flow ratio from 100% to 0% during the last loop which can in some cases lead to under extrusion at the end of the spiral.

parameters.label: Timelapse
parameters.description: If smooth or traditional mode is selected, a timelapse video will be generated for each print. After each layer is printed, a snapshot is taken with the chamber camera. All of these snapshots are composed into a timelapse video when printing completes. If smooth mode is selected, the toolhead will move to the excess chute after each layer is printed and then take a snapshot. Since the melt filament may leak from the nozzle during the process of taking a snapshot, prime tower is required for smooth mode to wipe nozzle.

parameters.label: Temperature variation
parameters.description: Temperature difference to be applied when an extruder is not active. The value is not used when 'idle_temperature' in filament settings is set to non zero value.

parameters.label: Preheat time
parameters.description: To reduce the waiting time after tool change, Orca can preheat the next tool while the current tool is still in use. This setting specifies the time in seconds to preheat the next tool. Orca will insert a M104 command to preheat the tool in advance.

parameters.label: Preheat steps
parameters.description: Insert multiple preheat commands(e.g. M104.1). Only useful for Prusa XL. For other printers, please set it to 1.

parameters.label: Start G-code
parameters.description: Start G-code when start the whole printing
parameters.default: G28 ; home all axes G1 Z5 F5000 ; lift nozzle

parameters.label: Start G-code
parameters.description: Start G-code when start the printing of this filament

parameters.label: Single Extruder Multi Material
parameters.description: Use single nozzle to print multi filament

parameters.label: Manual Filament Change
parameters.description: Enable this option to omit the custom Change filament G-code only at the beginning of the print. The tool change command (e.g., T0) will be skipped throughout the entire print. This is useful for manual multi-material printing, where we use M600/PAUSE to trigger the manual filament change action.

parameters.label: Purge in prime tower
parameters.description: Purge remaining filament into prime tower

parameters.label: Enable filament ramming
parameters.description: Enable filament ramming

parameters.label: No sparse layers (beta)
parameters.description: If enabled, the wipe tower will not be printed on layers with no toolchanges. On layers with a toolchange, extruder will travel downward to print the wipe tower. User is responsible for ensuring there is no collision with the print.

parameters.label: Prime all printing extruders
parameters.description: If enabled, all printing extruders will be primed at the front edge of the print bed at the start of the print.

parameters.label: Slice gap closing radius
parameters.description: Cracks smaller than 2x gap closing radius are being filled during the triangle mesh slicing. The gap closing operation may reduce the final print resolution, therefore it is advisable to keep the value reasonably low.

parameters.label: Slicing Mode
parameters.description: Use \ for 3DLabPrint airplane models. Use \ to close all holes in the model.

parameters.label: Z offset
parameters.description: This value will be added (or subtracted) from all the Z coordinates in the output G-code. It is used to compensate for bad Z endstop position: for example, if your endstop zero actually leaves the nozzle 0.3mm far from the print bed, set this to -0.3 (or fix your endstop).

parameters.label: Enable support
parameters.description: Enable support generation.

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: Normal (auto) and Tree (auto) is used to generate support automatically. If Normal (manual) or Tree (manual) is selected, only support enforcers are generated

parameters.label: Support/object xy distance
parameters.description: XY separation between an object and its support

parameters.label: Support/object first layer gap
parameters.description: XY separation between an object and its support at the first layer.

parameters.label: Pattern angle
parameters.description: Use this setting to rotate the support pattern on the horizontal plane.

parameters.label: On build plate only
parameters.description: Don't create support on model surface, only on build plate

parameters.label: Support critical regions only
parameters.description: Only create support for critical regions including sharp tail, cantilever, etc.

parameters.label: Remove small overhangs
parameters.description: Remove small overhangs that possibly need no supports.

parameters.label: Top Z distance
parameters.description: The z gap between the top support interface and object

parameters.label: Bottom Z distance
parameters.description: The z gap between the bottom support interface and object

parameters.label: Enforce support for the first
parameters.description: Generate support material for the specified number of layers counting from bottom, regardless of whether normal support material is enabled or not and regardless of any angle threshold. This is useful for getting more adhesion of objects having a very thin or poor footprint on the build plate.

parameters.label: Support/raft base
parameters.description: Filament to print support base and raft. \ means no specific filament for support and current filament is used

parameters.label: Avoid interface filament for base
parameters.description: Avoid using support interface filament to print support base if possible.

parameters.label: Support
parameters.description: Line width of support. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Interface use loop pattern
parameters.description: Cover the top contact layer of the supports with loops. Disabled by default.

parameters.label: Support/raft interface
parameters.description: Filament to print support interface. \ means no specific filament for support interface and current filament is used

parameters.label: Top interface layers
parameters.description: Number of top interface layers

parameters.label: Bottom interface layers
parameters.description: Number of bottom interface layers

parameters.label: Top interface spacing
parameters.description: Spacing of interface lines. Zero means solid interface

parameters.label: Bottom interface spacing
parameters.description: Spacing of bottom interface lines. Zero means solid interface

parameters.label: Support interface
parameters.description: Speed of support interface

parameters.label: Base pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of support

parameters.label: Interface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of support interface. Default pattern for non-soluble support interface is Rectilinear, while default pattern for soluble support interface is Concentric

parameters.label: Base pattern spacing
parameters.description: Spacing between support lines

parameters.label: Normal Support expansion
parameters.description: Expand (+) or shrink (-) the horizontal span of normal support

parameters.label: Support
parameters.description: Speed of support

parameters.label: Style
parameters.description: Style and shape of the support. For normal support, projecting the supports into a regular grid will create more stable supports (default), while snug support towers will save material and reduce object scarring. For tree support, slim and organic style will merge branches more aggressively and save a lot of material (default organic), while hybrid style will create similar structure to normal support under large flat overhangs.

parameters.label: Independent support layer height
parameters.description: Support layer uses layer height independent with object layer. This is to support customizing z-gap and save print time. This option will be invalid when the prime tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Threshold angle
parameters.description: Support will be generated for overhangs whose slope angle is below the threshold.

parameters.label: Threshold overlap
parameters.description: If threshold angle is zero, support will be generated for overhangs whose overlap is below the threshold. The smaller this value is, the steeper the overhang that can be printed without support.

parameters.label: Tree support branch angle
parameters.description: This setting determines the maximum overhang angle that t he branches of tree support allowed to make. If the angle is increased, the branches can be printed more horizontally, allowing them to reach farther.

parameters.label: Tree support branch angle
parameters.description: This setting determines the maximum overhang angle that t he branches of tree support allowed to make. If the angle is increased, the branches can be printed more horizontally, allowing them to reach farther.

parameters.label: Preferred Branch Angle
parameters.description: The preferred angle of the branches, when they do not have to avoid the model. Use a lower angle to make them more vertical and more stable. Use a higher angle for branches to merge faster.

parameters.label: Tree support branch distance
parameters.description: This setting determines the distance between neighboring tree support nodes.

parameters.label: Tree support branch distance
parameters.description: This setting determines the distance between neighboring tree support nodes.

parameters.label: Branch Density
parameters.description: Adjusts the density of the support structure used to generate the tips of the branches. A higher value results in better overhangs but the supports are harder to remove, thus it is recommended to enable top support interfaces instead of a high branch density value if dense interfaces are needed.

parameters.label: Adaptive layer height
parameters.description: Enabling this option means the height of  tree support layer except the first will be automatically calculated

parameters.label: Auto brim width
parameters.description: Enabling this option means the width of the brim for tree support will be automatically calculated

parameters.label: Tree support brim width
parameters.description: Distance from tree branch to the outermost brim line

parameters.label: Tip Diameter
parameters.description: Branch tip diameter for organic supports.

parameters.label: Tree support branch diameter
parameters.description: This setting determines the initial diameter of support nodes.

parameters.label: Branch Diameter Angle
parameters.description: The angle of the branches' diameter as they gradually become thicker towards the bottom. An angle of 0 will cause the branches to have uniform thickness over their length. A bit of an angle can increase stability of the organic support.

parameters.label: Tree support branch diameter
parameters.description: This setting determines the initial diameter of support nodes.

parameters.label: Support wall loops
parameters.description: This setting specifies the count of support walls in the range of [0,2]. 0 means auto.

parameters.label: Tree support with infill
parameters.description: This setting specifies whether to add infill inside large hollows of tree support

parameters.label: Activate temperature control
parameters.description: Enable this option for automated chamber temperature control. This option activates the emitting of an M191 command before the \ which sets the chamber temperature and waits until it is reached. In addition, it emits an M141 command at the end of the print to turn off the chamber heater, if present. This option relies on the firmware supporting the M191 and M141 commands either via macros or natively and is usually used when an active chamber heater is installed.

parameters.label: Chamber temperature
parameters.description: For high-temperature materials like ABS, ASA, PC, and PA, a higher chamber temperature can help suppress or reduce warping and potentially lead to higher interlayer bonding strength. However, at the same time, a higher chamber temperature will reduce the efficiency of air filtration for ABS and ASA. For PLA, PETG, TPU, PVA, and other low-temperature materials, this option should be disabled (set to 0) as the chamber temperature should be low to avoid extruder clogging caused by material softening at the heat break. If enabled, this parameter also sets a gcode variable named chamber_temperature, which can be used to pass the desired chamber temperature to your print start macro, or a heat soak macro like this: PRINT_START (other variables) CHAMBER_TEMP=[chamber_temperature]. This may be useful if your printer does not support M141/M191 commands, or if you desire to handle heat soaking in the print start macro if no active chamber heater is installed.

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Nozzle temperature for layers after the initial one

parameters.label: Detect thin wall
parameters.description: Detect thin wall which can't contain two line width. And use single line to print. Maybe printed not very well, because it's not closed loop

parameters.label: Change filament G-code
parameters.description: This gcode is inserted when change filament, including T command to trigger tool change

parameters.label: Change extrusion role G-code
parameters.description: This gcode is inserted when the extrusion role is changed

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Line width for top surfaces. If expressed as a %, it will be computed over the nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Speed of top surface infill which is solid

parameters.label: Top shell layers
parameters.description: This is the number of solid layers of top shell, including the top surface layer. When the thickness calculated by this value is thinner than top shell thickness, the top shell layers will be increased

parameters.label: Top shell thickness
parameters.description: The number of top solid layers is increased when slicing if the thickness calculated by top shell layers is thinner than this value. This can avoid having too thin shell when layer height is small. 0 means that this setting is disabled and thickness of top shell is absolutely determined by top shell layers

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: Speed of travel which is faster and without extrusion

parameters.label: Z travel
parameters.description: Speed of vertical travel along z axis. This is typically lower because build plate or gantry is hard to be moved. Zero means using travel speed directly in gcode, but will be limited by printer's ability when run gcode

parameters.label: Wipe while retracting
parameters.description: Move nozzle along the last extrusion path when retracting to clean leaked material on nozzle. This can minimize blob when print new part after travel

parameters.label: Wipe Distance
parameters.description: Describe how long the nozzle will move along the last path when retracting.   Depending on how long the wipe operation lasts, how fast and long the extruder/filament retraction settings are, a retraction move may be needed to retract the remaining filament.   Setting a value in the retract amount before wipe setting below will perform any excess retraction before the wipe, else it will be performed after.

parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: The wiping tower can be used to clean up the residue on the nozzle and stabilize the chamber pressure inside the nozzle, in order to avoid appearance defects when printing objects.

parameters.label: Purging volumes - load/unload volumes
parameters.description: This vector saves required volumes to change from/to each tool used on the wipe tower. These values are used to simplify creation of the full purging volumes below.

parameters.label: Purging volumes
parameters.description: This matrix describes volumes (in cubic milimetres) required to purge the new filament on the wipe tower for any given pair of tools.

parameters.label: Flush multiplier
parameters.description: The actual flushing volumes is equal to the flush multiplier multiplied by the flushing volumes in the table.

parameters.label: Prime volume
parameters.description: The volume of material to prime extruder on tower.

parameters.label: Position X
parameters.description: X coordinate of the left front corner of a wipe tower

parameters.label: Position Y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the left front corner of a wipe tower

parameters.label: Width
parameters.description: Width of prime tower

parameters.label: Wipe tower rotation angle
parameters.description: Wipe tower rotation angle with respect to x-axis.

parameters.label: Brim width
parameters.description: Brim width

parameters.label: Stabilization cone apex angle
parameters.description: Angle at the apex of the cone that is used to stabilize the wipe tower. Larger angle means wider base.

parameters.label: Maximum wipe tower print speed
parameters.description: The maximum print speed when purging in the wipe tower and printing the wipe tower sparse layers. When purging, if the sparse infill speed or calculated speed from the filament max volumetric speed is lower, the lowest will be used instead. When printing the sparse layers, if the internal perimeter speed or calculated speed from the filament max volumetric speed is lower, the lowest will be used instead. Increasing this speed may affect the tower's stability as well as increase the force with which the nozzle collides with any blobs that may have formed on the wipe tower. Before increasing this parameter beyond the default of 90mm/sec, make sure your printer can reliably bridge at the increased speeds and that ooze when tool changing is well controlled. For the wipe tower external perimeters the internal perimeter speed is used regardless of this setting.

parameters.label: Wipe tower
parameters.description: The extruder to use when printing perimeter of the wipe tower. Set to 0 to use the one that is available (non-soluble would be preferred).

parameters.label: Purging volumes - load/unload volumes
parameters.description: This vector saves required volumes to change from/to each tool used on the wipe tower. These values are used to simplify creation of the full purging volumes below.

parameters.label: Flush into objects' infill
parameters.description: Purging after filament change will be done inside objects' infills. This may lower the amount of waste and decrease the print time. If the walls are printed with transparent filament, the mixed color infill will be seen outside. It will not take effect, unless the prime tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Flush into objects' support
parameters.description: Purging after filament change will be done inside objects' support. This may lower the amount of waste and decrease the print time. It will not take effect, unless the prime tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Flush into this object
parameters.description: This object will be used to purge the nozzle after a filament change to save filament and decrease the print time. Colours of the objects will be mixed as a result. It will not take effect, unless the prime tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Maximal bridging distance
parameters.description: Maximal distance between supports on sparse infill sections.

parameters.label: Wipe tower purge lines spacing
parameters.description: Spacing of purge lines on the wipe tower.

parameters.label: Extra flow for purging
parameters.description: Extra flow used for the purging lines on the wipe tower. This makes the purging lines thicker or narrower than they normally would be. The spacing is adjusted automatically.

parameters.label: Idle temperature
parameters.description: Nozzle temperature when the tool is currently not used in multi-tool setups. This is only used when 'Ooze prevention' is active in Print Settings. Set to 0 to disable.

parameters.label: X-Y hole compensation
parameters.description: Holes of object will be grown or shrunk in XY plane by the configured value. Positive value makes holes bigger. Negative value makes holes smaller. This function is used to adjust size slightly when the object has assembling issue

parameters.label: X-Y contour compensation
parameters.description: Contour of object will be grown or shrunk in XY plane by the configured value. Positive value makes contour bigger. Negative value makes contour smaller. This function is used to adjust size slightly when the object has assembling issue

parameters.label: Convert holes to polyholes
parameters.description: Search for almost-circular holes that span more than one layer and convert the geometry to polyholes. Use the nozzle size and the (biggest) diameter to compute the polyhole. See http://hydraraptor.blogspot.com/2011/02/polyholes.html

parameters.label: Polyhole detection margin
parameters.description: Maximum defection of a point to the estimated radius of the circle. As cylinders are often exported as triangles of varying size, points may not be on the circle circumference. This setting allows you some leeway to broaden the detection. In mm or in % of the radius.

parameters.label: Polyhole twist
parameters.description: Rotate the polyhole every layer.

parameters.label: G-code thumbnails
parameters.description: Picture sizes to be stored into a .gcode and .sl1 / .sl1s files, in the following format: \

parameters.label: Format of G-code thumbnails
parameters.description: Format of G-code thumbnails: PNG for best quality, JPG for smallest size, QOI for low memory firmware

parameters.label: Use relative E distances
parameters.description: Relative extrusion is recommended when using \ option. Some extruders work better with this option unchecked (absolute extrusion mode). Wipe tower is only compatible with relative mode. It is recommended on most printers. Default is checked

parameters.label: Wall generator
parameters.description: Classic wall generator produces walls with constant extrusion width and for very thin areas is used gap-fill. Arachne engine produces walls with variable extrusion width

parameters.label: Wall transition length
parameters.description: When transitioning between different numbers of walls as the part becomes thinner, a certain amount of space is allotted to split or join the wall segments. It's expressed as a percentage over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Wall transitioning filter margin
parameters.description: Prevent transitioning back and forth between one extra wall and one less. This margin extends the range of extrusion widths which follow to [Minimum wall width - margin, 2 * Minimum wall width + margin]. Increasing this margin reduces the number of transitions, which reduces the number of extrusion starts/stops and travel time. However, large extrusion width variation can lead to under- or overextrusion problems. It's expressed as a percentage over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Wall transitioning threshold angle
parameters.description: When to create transitions between even and odd numbers of walls. A wedge shape with an angle greater than this setting will not have transitions and no walls will be printed in the center to fill the remaining space. Reducing this setting reduces the number and length of these center walls, but may leave gaps or overextrude

parameters.label: Wall distribution count
parameters.description: The number of walls, counted from the center, over which the variation needs to be spread. Lower values mean that the outer walls don't change in width

parameters.label: Minimum feature size
parameters.description: Minimum thickness of thin features. Model features that are thinner than this value will not be printed, while features thicker than the Minimum feature size will be widened to the Minimum wall width. It's expressed as a percentage over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Minimum wall length
parameters.description: Adjust this value to prevent short, unclosed walls from being printed, which could increase print time. Higher values remove more and longer walls. NOTE: Bottom and top surfaces will not be affected by this value to prevent visual gaps on the outside of the model. Adjust 'One wall threshold' in the Advanced settings below to adjust the sensitivity of what is considered a top-surface. 'One wall threshold' is only visible if this setting is set above the default value of 0.5, or if single-wall top surfaces is enabled.

parameters.label: First layer minimum wall width
parameters.description: The minimum wall width that should be used for the first layer is recommended to be set to the same size as the nozzle. This adjustment is expected to enhance adhesion.

parameters.label: Minimum wall width
parameters.description: Width of the wall that will replace thin features (according to the Minimum feature size) of the model. If the Minimum wall width is thinner than the thickness of the feature, the wall will become as thick as the feature itself. It's expressed as a percentage over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Detect narrow internal solid infill
parameters.description: This option will auto detect narrow internal solid infill area. If enabled, concentric pattern will be used for the area to speed printing up. Otherwise, rectilinear pattern is used by default.
